headline: Teacher Transfer System Separates 28,000 Families Annually
context: MOE's centralized transfer system moves teachers to new locations often separating them from spouses and children.

cards.big: 28,000 teachers live apart from families due to inter-state postings in 2025
cards.sub: Average separation period is 4.6 years before transfer approval

cards.big: Teacher morale index drops 34% among those posted more than 200km from family
cards.sub: Separated teachers show higher absenteeism and lower student evaluation scores

cards.big: Transfer application backlog reached 85,000 pending requests as of December 2025
cards.sub: Processing capacity handles only 33% of annual requests creating multi-year waits

cards.big: Japan rotates teachers every 3-6 years within the same prefecture keeping families together
cards.sub: Regional rotation achieves equitable distribution without prolonged family separation

cards.big: Teachers who are worrying about their distant families cannot give their best to students
Should teacher postings be managed at state level to minimize family separations
